behaviors.description: Out-of-stock products are excluded even if they would score highly.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because failure is at module handoff between inventory-availability filtering and ranking output assembly ('excluded ... regardless of relevance score'). Integration vs System -> Integration wins because behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess. Integration-brake: (a) no real infra required, (b) no AI/ML output-quality dependence stated, (c) no multi-agent chain, (d) no LLM-stage orchestration. Rejection gate: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior only describes local recommendation pipeline logic with availability flags, not external infra or model judgments.
behaviors.test_description: Wire recommender service + catalog module + availability filter + ranker in-process (local test DB or fixtures). Seed one out-of-stock item with highest score and verify it never appears in returned recommendations.

behaviors.description: Blocked categories in user settings are excluded.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because bug appears when user-settings data is handed to category filter. Integration vs System -> Integration wins: no OS/network/subprocess requirement in behavior text. Integration-brake checks all negative for System/Agentic/Workflow. Rejection gate: higher levels disqualified by behavior being settings-to-filter wiring, not real infra or AI output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process user-profile/settings module plus filtering module. Create blocked categories in settings, run recommendation request, assert no returned product belongs to blocked categories.

behaviors.description: Products matching recent browsing patterns rank above products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because ranking depends on combining browsing-feature extraction with scoring/ranking module, not one isolated function. Integration vs System -> Integration wins since behavior does not require real HTTP/OS/subprocess. Counterbalance: no LLM or non-deterministic model decisions in text. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because behavior describes deterministic recommendation signals ('browsing patterns') rather than real model output quality checks.
behaviors.test_description: Assemble browsing-history reader + feature builder + ranker in-process. Seed candidate products where only one group has browsing signal; verify ranked order puts signaled products ahead.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns correct page with stable ordering across repeated page-2 requests when data is unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because stability depends on interaction of sorter + paginator + request parameters. Integration vs System -> Integration wins because no real infra requirement is stated. Integration-brake: no OS/network/subprocess, no AI/ML dependence, no multi-agent chain. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior is about deterministic ordering/pagination logic inside service modules.
behaviors.test_description: Run in-process recommendation pipeline with deterministic fixture data and explicit tie cases. Request page 2 twice without mutating data; assert identical IDs and order both times and correct offset/limit boundaries.

behaviors.description: At least one recommendation comes from an unbrowsed category (serendipity).
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because serendipity constraint is enforced when diversification logic is combined with base ranking and user-history inputs. Integration vs System -> Integration wins: no real OS/network/subprocess required by text. Counterbalance: not Agentic; behavior does not mention LLM/ML API call, only recommendation algorithm rules. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model-judgment pipeline is specified; requirement is algorithmic constraint enforcement.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process ranker + diversification component + browsing-history input. Seed catalog with strong same-category relevance and at least one alternative category; assert final page includes >=1 product from an unbrowsed category.

behaviors.description: Cold-start users get popularity-based recommendations instead of empty results.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because failure appears at handoff between user-history lookup (empty) and fallback popularity strategy. Integration vs System -> Integration wins; no real infra needed in behavior text. Integration-brake: no external OS/network, no AI-output dependency, no multi-step agent chain. Rejection gate: rejected higher levels because behavior explicitly targets local fallback policy ('based on overall popularity').
behaviors.test_description: Wire user-history service + recommender + popularity source in-process. Provide new user with no events and non-empty popularity data; assert non-empty recommendations sourced from popularity ranking.

behaviors.description: Click tracking captures product ID, list position, and timestamp for each clicked recommendation.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because correctness depends on request context (rank position) being passed to tracking persistence. Integration vs System -> Integration wins for minimum boundary: can be caught with in-process modules plus local datastore/clock abstraction; behavior text does not require real network/subprocess. Integration-brake: no AI/ML or multi-agent chain. Rejection gate: considered System for timestamp realism, rejected because behavior does not require real OS time source; deterministic clock injection is sufficient to catch handoff bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Execute recommendation response generation and click-event recording together in-process with test DB. Simulate a click on a known list position and assert stored event has exact product ID, position, and expected timestamp.

behaviors.description: Recommendation generation stays under 200ms for a 100,000-product catalog.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins first because latency emerges from full pipeline interaction. Integration vs System -> System wins because timing requirement depends on real runtime/OS scheduling/memory behavior at realistic scale; in-process fakes can hide regressions. Counterbalance: System vs Agentic -> System wins since text is latency SLO, not model output quality. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic/Workflow because no LLM/ML non-deterministic decision quality is required by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real recommendation service process (or full executable path) against a realistic 100k-product dataset on representative hardware/runtime settings. Measure end-to-end request latency and assert p95 (or specified metric) remains <200ms.

behaviors.description: After user purchases a recommended product, that product is deprioritized in later recommendations.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because bug is in state propagation between purchase-event ingestion and subsequent ranking. Integration vs System -> Integration wins because behavior text does not require real network/subprocess/OS features. Integration-brake: no AI-output-quality dependence, no multi-agent sequence. Rejection gate: rejected higher levels because behavior is local state-update + ranking interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process purchase-history update path and recommendation ranker. First request yields product X; record purchase of X; second request with updated state should rank X lower (or below control products).

behaviors.description: Recommendations are deterministic for unchanged user state across repeated requests.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because determinism depends on combined behavior of feature retrieval, sort keys, and pagination/ranking assembly across modules. Integration vs System -> Integration wins since behavior text does not require real external infra. Integration-brake: no LLM/ML content-quality dependency and no multi-agent chain. Rejection gate: considered System for concurrency/randomness sources, rejected because behavior only specifies repeated requests with no intervening activity and can be validated in-process by controlling seeds/time.
behaviors.test_description: Run full in-process recommendation pipeline twice for identical user snapshot and catalog snapshot with fixed random seed/config. Assert identical ordered product IDs across both responses.
